title: Import + seed: WPS register, welder quals, op library, routing templates
description:
Import scripts (Node/TS, one-shot):
1. assets/Welding Control Register (rev 2) updated DECEMBER 25.xlsx — sheet 'Weld Procedure Records - WPQR' → wps table (~20 rows). Sheet 'WELDER QUAL RANGE' → welder_qualifications (group by welder name, join to employees by name match — warn on misses).
2. Seed op_library with the 26 codes from docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, each tagged with required_skill + default_hold_point_after.
3. Seed routing_templates: Fabrication / Machining / Assembly / Site Install. Plus finishing-appendable ops as a separate category.

Idempotent — re-runnable without dupes. Script path: app/scripts/import-wps.ts, seed-op-library.ts, seed-routing-templates.ts.

title: Supabase migrations: production_card data model
description:
Create migrations under app/migrations/ for the data model defined in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Data model section). Tables:
- production_cards (state machine, dual-signoff columns, doc_id, doc_number, card_rev, exc_class denorm, supersedes self-FK)
- card_parts (drawing meta, material_spec, FKs to purchase_orders + document_matl_cert nullable)
- card_part_ops (seq, op_code FK, required_skill, hold_point_after, wps_id nullable, state)
- production_card_events (kiosk_id, employee_id, card_part_op_id, action enum, consumable_heat nullable, meta jsonb, ts)
- card_op_sessions (view derived from events: pair start+stop, duration)
- card_inspections (level_iso5817, defects jsonb, photo_ids jsonb, signer)
- card_signoffs (role, type, employee_id, signed_at, notes)
- op_library (code PK, label, required_skill, default_hold_point_after, category)
- routing_templates + routing_template_ops (process_family, seq → op_code)
- wps (full attributes from Welding Control Register sheet 3)
- welder_qualifications (welder_employee_id FK, wps_id_or_range, expires_at, status, qualification_doc_id)

Also: ALTER employees ADD COLUMN skill_set text[], auth_user_id uuid REFERENCES auth_users(id). ALTER project_register ADD COLUMN exc_class smallint (if not already present).

RLS policies for each table: authenticated read; role-gated writes per the role matrix in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
notes:
Table names per docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (updated 2026-05-11 after naming review): production_card / production_card_part / production_card_part_op / production_card_event / production_card_op_session (view) / production_card_inspection / production_card_signoff / welding_wps / welding_welder_qualification / production_op_library / production_routing_template / production_routing_template_op. Earlier draft used unprefixed names (wps, op_library, etc.) — do NOT use those.
Update 2026-05-11: (1) employees gets 'role text[]' (NOT skill_set). (2) project_register_items gets 'exc_class smallint' (NOT project_register). (3) production_card_part gets 'exc_class smallint' (denormed at issue). (4) production_card has NO exc_class column. Card-level UIs derive max exc_class via JOIN to parts.
Schema update 2026-05-11: production_card identity is FK-only — doc_id (→document_incoming_scan.id) and project_register_item_id (→project_register_items.id). Do NOT add doc_number or project_number text columns on production_card; resolve via JOIN. exc_class is on production_card (single column denormed from project_register_items at issue). production_card_part has neither exc_class nor project_register_item_id (both inherited from card). One card = one project_register_items row.

title: Wire /production-card/ route in platform-portal nginx production.conf
description: Add location /production-card/ block and upstream/set $ entries in platform-portal/docker/nginx/production.conf pointing at http://production-card:3014. Rebuild + push the gateway image. Deploy gateway AFTER the production-card app is up on 3014 to avoid a proxy gap.

title: Scaffold app/ (Next.js + basePath + Dockerfile + compose + build.sh)
description:
Per platform-portal/docs/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md, create:
- app/package.json
- app/next.config.ts (basePath: '/production-card', output: 'standalone')
- app/tsconfig.json
- app/Dockerfile (multi-stage, EXPOSE 3014, non-root user)
- app/.dockerignore (MANDATORY — excludes host node_modules + .next)
- app/app/layout.tsx + page.tsx skeleton
- docker-compose.app.yml (service: production-card, port 3014, joins platform_net, env_file ../platform-portal/.env)
- build.sh (buildx → ghcr.io/ukstevem/production-card with --build-arg for NEXT_PUBLIC_*)
- Copy frozen packages: cp -r ../platform-portal/packages/{ui,auth,supabase} app/packages/. and strip workspace:* deps.

Acceptance: 'docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d' starts the container, http://localhost:3014/production-card/ returns the placeholder page.

title: Reserve port 3014 + service name in platform-portal/docs/PORTS.md
description:
Add the row for production-card to platform-portal/docs/PORTS.md and the matching upstream + location block to platform-portal/docker/nginx/production.conf. Open a PR or push directly. Port 3014 is currently the next reserved slot.

| Port | Service | App | Route | Source |
| 3014 | production-card | Production Card | /production-card/ | pss-production-card/app/ |

Blocks pc-002 and all downstream MVP work — without the port reserved, nginx routing will collide.

title: Web: lightweight inspections + final inspection + close flow
description:
Inspector + QC facing flows:

Op-level inspections (per part-op at hold points):
1. /production-card/[id]/parts/[partId]/ops/[opId]/inspect — pass/fail/rework radio, free-text note, signer (from auth_users), submitted_at. EXC ≥ 3 unlocks structured panel (defects per ISO 5817 levels with location dropdown, photo upload count, dim record link).
2. On fail/rework: op state → rework; planner can add a new op or repeat existing.
3. On pass: op state → accepted.

Final inspection (per card, after all ops accepted):
1. State auto-transitions to awaiting_final_inspection when all ops on all parts are accepted.
2. /production-card/[id]/final-inspect — full traveller review, dim check, paint DFT, marking. Inspector signs.
3. On pass: state → complete.

Close:
1. QC clicks 'Close' on a complete card. SoD check: cannot be the issuer.
2. card_signoffs row inserted (role=qc, type=close).
3. Traveller PDF regenerated with all evidence + refiled to doc service.
4. State → closed (immutable).

Acceptance: a card moves draft → issued → in_progress → awaiting_final → complete → closed with all evidence captured.

title: Auto-close production-card sessions on shift-end clock-out
description:
Reactive trigger: when an employee's tap in timecard_events flips them to 'out' status (parity rule), close any open production_card_events session for that employee_id.

Implementation: Supabase trigger or scheduled function (whichever fits the existing patterns in pss-employee-presence). Insert a synthetic production_card_events row with action='stop' and meta={reason:'auto_shift_end'}.

Edge cases:
- Employee clocks out then back in same day → existing session stays closed; new scan starts a new session
- Employee taps but pre-flight fails (welder qual expired) → no session opened, nothing to close
- Multiple cards open (shouldn't happen per Q7(a), but defensively): close all

Acceptance: an open weld session at 5pm gets a stop event when the employee clocks out at 5:30pm; the card_op_sessions view shows the closed session with the correct duration.

title: Kiosk firmware (M5Stack RFID + barcode) + HMAC POST + weld pre-flight
description:
Firmware in firmware/kiosk/ (PlatformIO, ESP32-S3/M5Stack core). Mirror pss-employee-presence/firmware/roll-call/ pattern. Boot → WiFi → idle screen.

Flow on tap+scan:
1. RFID tap → POST /api/kiosk/resolve-operator with {card_id} → returns {employee_id, name, skill_set}
2. Display 'Hi {name}' + 'Scan a part QR'
3. Barcode scan → POST /api/kiosk/scan {employee_id, qr_payload, kiosk_id, hmac}
4. Backend logic (in app/, not firmware):
   a. Parse qr_payload (card or card/part code)
   b. If card-level: prompt operator to pick part (1-tap)
   c. If part-level: find next pending op on this part matching operator's skill_set
   d. If exactly one → start (insert production_card_events action='start'). If multiple → return list, kiosk renders 1-tap pick.
   e. WELD PRE-FLIGHT: if op_code='weld' — check welder qual current for op's wps_id (fail closed if not qualified or expired). Prompt for consumable_heat scan/entry. Validate against WPS consumable_spec.
   f. Auto-close any prior open session for this employee.
5. Kiosk displays 'Started: {op} on {part}. Tap again to stop.'

HMAC: shared secret from KIOSK_HMAC_SECRET env, SHA256 of (timestamp + body). Reject events older than 5 minutes (replay protection).

Out of scope here: WiFi config UI (use hard-coded include/config.h.example pattern from roll-call).
notes: Naming correction 2026-05-11: 'skill_set' is now 'role' (column on employees). The kiosk resolve-operator endpoint should return {employee_id, name, role}. The skill_set filter logic in step 4c becomes 'matching operator's role'. Op-library and routing column is 'required_role' not 'required_skill'. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

title: Web: WPS register CRUD + welder qualifications CRUD
description:
Welding-engineer and QC facing screens:
1. /production-card/admin/wps — list, create, edit, archive WPS records. All attrs from sheet 3 of Welding Control Register: wps_no, standard, process, material_grade, thickness_range, position, joint_type, consumable_spec, gas_flux, mode_of_transfer, preheat, charpy_test, plus a range-of-qualification block.
2. /production-card/admin/welder-quals — list, create, edit, deactivate welder qualifications. Welder picker (employees with welder skill), WPS picker, expires_at date, qualification_doc_id (FK to documents app — link, do not upload here).
3. List screens highlight quals expiring within 30 days (orange) and expired (red).
4. Role: welding_engineer or qc.

Acceptance: WPS imported by pc-005 are listed; new WPS can be created; welder Bob can have his PSS 031A qual marked as expiring 2026-09-01; the kiosk pre-flight (pc-009) can query 'is Bob qualified for WPS 031A today'.

title: Web: dual e-signoff issue flow → mint 61355 + file traveller PDF
description:
Issue button on draft cards. Flow:
1. Planner clicks 'Submit for QC review' → state stays draft but locks routing
2. QC reviews + clicks 'Issue' → both signatures captured into card_signoffs (role=planner type=issue + role=qc type=issue)
3. On dual signoff: state → issued. Card immutable from this point except for the rev mechanism.
4. App POSTs traveller PDF to pss-document-service /api/file with iso_description_id=66 (shop) or 67 (site), project_number=card.project_number. Stores returned id + doc_number on the card row.
5. PDF generation: cover sheet with QR + header (project, customer, exc_class, dates), part pages each with QR + drawing meta + routing ops + signoff blocks. Library: @react-pdf/renderer or playwright→PDF. Decide at impl time.
6. Re-issue path (card_rev > 1): refile via doc-service refile_override endpoint with -r2 suffix.

Acceptance: a draft card with 2 parts can be dual-signed, gets a doc_number like '10358-M&DC-0001', and the PDF is retrievable via the doc service URL.

title: Web: project picker → card draft → parts → routing editor
description:
Planner-facing flow. Screens:
1. /production-card — landing: list of cards, filterable by state + project + exc_class
2. /production-card/new — project picker (autocomplete project_register), variant pick (shop|site), creates draft card
3. /production-card/[id] — draft view: add parts (drawing_no, rev, qty, description, weight, material_spec free-text + optional FK pickers for purchase_orders / document_matl_cert)
4. /production-card/[id]/parts/[partId]/routing — per-part routing editor: pick template → ops added with seq, planner can reorder, add finishing ops, set wps_id for weld ops, mark hold-points

State stays 'draft' throughout. No issue button here — that's pc-007.
Auth: planner role required (use jobcard_project_members or new production_card_project_members).

value: production_card scopes to exactly ONE project_register_items row. Identity FKs only: production_card.doc_id → document_incoming_scan.id (resolves doc_number via JOIN), production_card.project_register_item_id → project_register_items.id (resolves projectnumber + item_seq via JOIN). Do NOT store doc_number or project_number as text columns on production_card. exc_class is a single column on production_card (denormed from project_register_items at issue time, applies to all parts on the card). production_card_part has NO exc_class and NO project_register_item_id — both inherited from card.

EXC class lives on project_register_items.exc_class (per item, not per project — items on a single project can differ). Denormed to production_card_part.exc_class at card issue. Drop any reference to project_register.exc_class or production_card.exc_class — those were earlier drafts. Pre-flight rules and inspection-fidelity (lightweight vs structured EXC3+) apply per part-op using the part's exc_class. Also: employees.skill_set was renamed to employees.role text[] (operator trades — welder/fabricator/painter/fitter/inspector/qc/site).

Initial design grilled and locked 2026-05-11. Port 3014, route /production-card/, service production-card. Op resolution at kiosk is IMPLICIT — operator skill + part state picks the next pending op, no menu. Welding pre-flight at weld scan-in is the lever for low-friction EXC2/3 compliance. WPS + welder quals canonical here; consumables stay on monday.com short-term then move to matl-cert. Doc numbering via pss-document-service 61355: M&DC, desc 66 shop / 67 site, shared serial pool per project. Card states: draft → issued (dual signoff planner+qc) → in_progress → awaiting_final → complete → closed (immutable). SoD enforced: same person can't operate+accept same op, can't issue+final-inspect same card.

Table naming locked 2026-05-11. Card-scoped: production_card, production_card_part, production_card_part_op, production_card_event, production_card_op_session (view), production_card_inspection, production_card_signoff. Welding registers: welding_wps, welding_welder_qualification. Production registers: production_op_library, production_routing_template, production_routing_template_op. Column adds: employees.skill_set text[], employees.auth_user_id uuid NULL FK, project_register.exc_class smallint. Conventions: snake_case singular; card-scoped prefixed production_card_; welding registers welding_; generic production registers production_; no v_ prefix on views.
